summary: The test suite has good structure and organization with clear separation between unit tests (test_bumper.py) and integration tests (test_workflow.py). Unit tests cover core functions (parse_commits, bump_version, read/write_version) adequately. However, significant gaps exist: generate_changelog has only 1 test despite complex formatting logic; process() has only 1 end-to-end scenario; workflow tests use loose substring matching instead of validating actual output structure. Fixture files (fixtures/feat.log, fix.log, breaking.log, noop.log) are referenced but not shown—if missing, tests cannot run. The 4 act test cases provide basic scenario coverage (feat→minor, fix→patch, breaking→major, noop) but lack edge cases like multiple scopes, perf-type commits, version 0.x.x, or mixed commit types. Workflow structure validation is thorough (actionlint, triggers, checkout). The test design itself is clean (temp directories, clear naming, proper isolation) and demonstrates TDD intent, but execution has incomplete coverage of requirements.
